description: 60F, T2DM on metformin, A1C 8.2%, established ASCVD (prior MI), eGFR 62. SGLT2i for cardiorenal benefit (R2). GLP-1 RA for CVD benefit (R3). Intensification (R4, A1C ≥7%). Statin high-intensity (R5).

actions.label: Initiate SGLT2 inhibitor for cardiorenal benefit
actions.rationale: ADA-A: Patient has T2DM with established ASCVD (prior MI) and borderline CKD (eGFR 62). SGLT2i recommended independent of A1C for cardiorenal protection. Empagliflozin, dapagliflozin, or canagliflozin.

actions.label: Initiate GLP-1 receptor agonist for cardiovascular benefit
actions.rationale: ADA-A: Patient has T2DM with established ASCVD. GLP-1 RA with proven CVD benefit (semaglutide, liraglutide, dulaglutide) recommended. Can be used with SGLT2i for additive benefit.

actions.label: Intensify glycemic therapy (A1C above target)
actions.rationale: ADA-A: A1C 8.2% is above the 7% target despite metformin. Add SGLT2i (addresses cardiorenal risk too), GLP-1 RA (addresses ASCVD risk), or both. Insulin if A1C remains high.

actions.label: Initiate high-intensity statin therapy
actions.rationale: ADA-A: Diabetic adult age 40-75 with established ASCVD and LDL 128 mg/dL ≥100. Multiple risk factors warrant high-intensity statin (atorvastatin 40-80 mg or rosuvastatin 20-40 mg) to achieve ≥50% LDL-C reduction.

contraindications.label: Continue metformin alone without escalation
contraindications.rationale: ADA-A: A1C 8.2% above target and established ASCVD require intensification with agents that have proven cardiovascular benefit.
